description:
With `-o backend=fskit` and macFUSE 5.2.0 on macOS 26.4.1, the mount succeeds (mount table shows the `fskit` flag) but the daemon segfaults on the first directory access.

Crash (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/turnkey-composed-2026-04-29-14*.ips):
  EXC_BAD_ACCESS, KERN_INVALID_ADDRESS at 0x00000000000120a8
  Faulting thread: composition::fuse::fuse_t::operations::fuse_readdir
    -> calling addr 0x120a8 (unmapped)
  Stack: fuse_session_loop_mt -> fuse_session_process_buf_internal -> fuse_readdir

Diagnosis: macFUSE FSKit's libfuse announces 'FUSE library version: 3.18.2' but our hand-written bindings target 3.16 (FUSE_MAJOR=3 / FUSE_MINOR=16 in bindings.rs). The readdir filler argument under FSKit is non-null but is NOT a function pointer (probably an opaque handle / index). Rust's Option<fn> just discriminates null vs non-null, so my earlier 'if Some, call it' guard sees Some(0x120a8) and tries to call it -> segfault.

Sole blocker for a working FSKit mount on Tahoe. The mount path itself (fuse_mount, opendir, lookup, getattr, mkdir-EROFS) all work; only readdir crashes.

Workarounds to evaluate:
  1. Skip ops.readdir under FSKit (detect via detect_macfuse_backend) and rely on LOOKUP-per-entry for enumeration.
  2. Update bindings to libfuse 3.18 fuse_operations layout - re-check struct size and per-callback signatures, especially fuse_fill_dir_t.
  3. Switch from the high-level fuse_operations API to the low-level fuse_lowlevel.h API, which may be more directly mapped to FSKit semantics.
title: fuse_readdir segfaults under macFUSE FSKit (libfuse3.18 ABI mismatch)
